post.content:
In a Binary maze with 0 and 1, 0 is the valid cell to which we can travel and 1 means that the cell is blocked. Given source and destination. We have to find- 
1. IF path exists, if yes, find shortest path. 
2. If we are given a chance to toggle single cell from 1 to 0 , which cell you will toggle so that you will surely get the shortest path.
How to tackle the second part without toggling each cell.
Is there a better way to do the same?

cool question. found good answer here: https://www.quora.com/Given-start-and-target-position-of-a-robot-on-a-2D-grid-with-obstacles-What-is-the-shortest-path-length-if-robot-can-remove-at-most-3-obstacles\n\nbasically just do standard BFS, and consider removing an obstacle as a free (0-length) move in an imaginary Z direction. now it is easy to generalize for removal of N obstacles
